characters.name: Zach Adanto
characters.description: Zach AdantoGenderMaleLocationDenver, ColoradoStatusAliveFirst AppearancePast Imperfect Zach Adantois a former colleague ofMyka's and, in 2011, Director of Investigations for the Denver Office of the Secret Service. Zach comes back into Myka's life when she spotsLeowhilst on aWarehousemission in Denver; Myka's feels that catchingSam's killer will give her necessary closure, and enlists the help of the local office, who happen to be her former colleagues before she got the promotion to D.C. Zach soon becomes Myka andPete's prime suspect for the accomplice who, they discover, set Sam up to die at Leo's hand. In the end, however, it's Zach who saves the Warehouse agents, when his time-frozen bullet thaws just asJim, the real traitor, is in its path. Zach and Myka part on better terms than previously, and Pete demonstrates once more that he is the best partner she's ever had.Past Imperfect

characters.name: Amy
characters.description: AmyGenderFemaleStatusAlivePlayed byAmanda BrugelFirst AppearanceThe Big SnagLast AppearanceThe Big Snag Amyis a villainous saleswoman and a car thief from "The Big Snag." Amy works as a saleswoman at a car dealership that has been selling stolen cars. She encountersArtie,Claudia, andSteveand is asked about a mysterious thief, who has been stealing cars and returning them to the dealership, but she sends them to her boss, who claims that the thief merely contacts him and gets him any car he wants.

characters.name: Amy

characters.name: James Aquino
characters.description: James AquinoGenderMaleRaceCaucasianAffiliationWalter SykesStatusDeceasedPlayed byNoah DanbyFirst Appearance"The 40th Floor" James Aquino was a demolitions expert who was wanted in nine countries. He was skilled in hand-to-hand combat and firearms. He favored molotovs and pipe bombs as his brands of explosives. His psychological abilities were espionage, political instigation, riot instigation, coercion, information extraction, and inciting dissident activity. James Aquino was an associate ofSally Stukowskiand by extension,Walter Sykes. He carried theBerlin Wall Spray Paintand used it on the building housing the Regent meeting. Aquino was discovered at a bank, trying to move money to a specific account. Aquino spottedPeteandClaudiain a reflection in a lamp and took the banker hostage. He sprayed on the banker's jacket with the spray paint, causing it to disintegrate, which gave Aquino enough time to get to his car.

characters.name: Regent Archer
characters.description: Lok ArcherRegentGenderMaleRaceChineseLocationUnknownAffiliationWarehouse 13StatusDeceasedPlayed byLok ArcherFirst Appearance"Breakdown"Last Appearance"Shadows" LokArcherwas a Regent present during the meeting betweenArtieandthe Regentsin "Breakdown". He changed the sign on the diner door to "Closed" and discouraged would-be customers. He was also atMyka Bering's reinstatement hearing, where he participated withTheadora StantonandAdwin Kosanin determining whether it would be suitable for her to return to work.[1]Unlike Ted, his job is never specified. He was tortured for information byWalter Sykesand subsequently killed, but not before he provided a journal belonging toCaturanga[2]that contained information about the location to the Regents' Sanctum.[3]

characters.name: Artifact Bearer
characters.description: An artifact bearer is a person who through some means or other has come into possession of an artifact. Warehouse agents must face them when they misuse the artifact or to simply acquire the artifact. They are various people from all walks of life and can be generally sorted into four types: These are people who have discovered they have an artifact and use it for self-serving and evil purposes. They are usually defeated by the agents, and sometimes by the artifact itself, which can turn on the user or be employed against them.

characters.name: Carol Augustine
characters.description: Carol AugustineCarol AugustineGenderFemaleLocationWashington, D.C.StatusAliveRelationshipsArthur NielsonJames MacPhersonFirst Appearance"Implosion" InMacPherson, it was stated that fifteen years previouslyJames MacPhersonsaved Carol with thePhoenix, causing five firemen to die in their place. She clearly has a long and complicated history with both Artie and MacPherson, but with the latter's death this is never explored further in the series.

characters.name: Carol Augustine
characters.description: Carol AugustineCarol AugustineGenderFemaleLocationWashington, D.C.StatusAliveRelationshipsArthur NielsonJames MacPhersonFirst Appearance"Implosion" InMacPherson, it was stated that fifteen years previouslyJames MacPhersonsaved Carol with thePhoenix, causing five firemen to die in their place. characters.name: Cody Bell
characters.description: Cody Bellis a former soldier and character encountered in the episode "Second Chance". He is also one of the few examples of a person who bonded with an artifact. While on duty in Iraq with the army, Cody was caught up in an explosion at a local museum. He survived, but ended up with shrapnel logged near his heart that doctors were unable to remove. Unknown to all, the shrapnel was not from the bomb but rather the remains of the Spartan Armor Artifact that had been caught in the explosion. Upon returning from military service, he started working at the local steel mill. One day he and his father were caught in the rafters when the lift they were on malfunctioned. His father was thrown from the lift and would have fallen to his death had Cody not caught him. Cody's determination to save his father's life activated the shrapnel shard, giving him the strength to pull his father to safety.

characters.name: Bonnie Belski
characters.description: Bonnie BelskiAgentGenderFemaleRaceCaucasianLocationChicago, ILAffiliationFBIPlayed byTricia HelferFirst Appearance"Resonance"  Bonnie is a FBI agent based in Chicago. She knows all about the past ofPeteandMyka. She does not trust them and even stated that they leave disaster in their wake. She was given the lead agent role on a string of robberies that were happening at banks in the city.

characters.name: Benedict Valda
characters.description: Benedict ValdaRegentGenderMaleRaceWhiteStatusDeceased (In the original timeline);Erased from Existence (Alternate Reality)Played byMark SheppardFirst Appearance"Breakdown"Last Appearance"Cangku Shisi" Benedict Valdawas one ofthe Regentsof theWarehouse. He might have been categorized as the Regents’ “attack dog” as he had spearheaded the Regent inquisition conducted forArthur Nielsen, was a seemingly constant critic of the Warehouse's agents (and to a lesser extent, even its Caretaker). Regardless of his cynical disposition, he was completely committed to his work for the Warehouse. Valda was also the Regents' expert onWarehouse 2and led agentsPete Lattimer,Myka Bering, andH. G. Wellsthrough it until his death in an act of self-sacrifice, a choice which made it possible for the others to continue and complete the mission, thereby saving both Warehouse 13 and, by extension its current caretaker,Mrs. Frederic.

characters.name: Jeannie Bering
characters.description: Jeannie BeringJeannie BeringGenderFemaleRaceCaucasianLocationColorado Springs, COAffiliationBering and Sons BookstoreStatusAliveFamilyWarren Bering(Husband)Tracy Bering (Daughter)Myka Bering(Daughter)Played bySusan HoganFirst Appearance"Nevermore"Last Appearance"The Greatest Gift" The mother ofMyka Bering, and Tracy, and wife of Warren Bering who along with her husband owns a bookstore. Jeannie obviously attempts to mediate between her stubborn husband and equally headstrong eldest daughter, but appears to have consistently taken the side of the former when the chips were down. She herself is the near-victim ofJack the Ripper's Lanternwhen it is used byJames MacPhersonto blackmail Myka and Pete into handing overEdgar Allan Poe's Quill Pen and Notebook. She takes strongly to Pete, as does her husband, and seems genuinely concerned for Myka, although the two of them appear to communicate little better than father and daughter.

characters.name: Myka Bering
characters.actor_name: Joanne KellyEddie McClintock(Body Switch)Jaime Murray(in disguise)
characters.description: Myka Ophelia BeringAgent Myka Ophelia BeringSpecial AgentWarehouse AgentGenderFemaleAliases or NicknamesMykesBunnySlimOphelia TeetonsMyka Burton(alias; "Age Before Beauty")MykamusMaribel Obregón (Salvaje Seducción)Barb(alias; "Endless" deleted scene)LocationBed and Breakfast(Univille, SD)AffiliationUnited States Secret ServiceWarehouse 13GradeGS-14PartnerPeter LattimerQuirksKeen sense of observation and perceptionLove of literatureMultilingual: French, Portuguese, Latin, Russian, ChineseFamilyWarren Bering(Father)Jeannie Bering(Mother)Tracy Bering (Sister)Kevin (Brother-in-Law)Unnamed NiblingUnnamed Uncle (deceased)Cindy (Aunt or Great Aunt)RelationshipsSam Martino(formerly)Helena WellsPeter Lattimer(ongoing)Played byJoanne KellyEddie McClintock(Body Switch)Jaime Murray(in disguise)First Appearance"Pilot"Last Appearance"Endless" Myka Ophelia[1]Beringis an agent of theUnited States Secret Servicestationed atWarehouse 13inUniville, South Dakota. She is the professional and romantic partner ofPete Lattimer. Myka likes puzzles[24]and skating because it helps her think.[7]She does not eat sugar except under stress, which likely stems from her strong desire to do things by the book and retain a considerable amount of control over every aspect of her life. Her compulsiveness and need for control likely stem from Sam's influence and subsequent death, and what she felt was her part in it. She used to be emotional, impulsive and quick to action, but has changed since she worked within that particular Secret Service office. Myka has also begun to relax after working at the Warehouse indicating she may have found a balance between who she is and who she was. She also has a fear of heights (as mentioned in "The 40th Floor") and a fear of creatures that have tentacles such as jellyfish and octopi.[29][30]Myka also seems to have a soft spot for animals since she took in the ferretPeteand, acording to Pete inThe Greatest Gift, "can't watch a movie where an animal dies." In the first two seasons, she also has a strange obsession with "Twizzlers".

characters.name: Warren Bering
characters.description: Warren BeringWarren BeringProprietor ofBering and Sons BookstoreGenderMaleRaceCaucasianLocationColorado Springs, COAffiliationBering and Sons BookstoreFamilyJeannie Bering(Wife)Tracy Bering (Daughter)Myka Bering(Daughter)Played byMichael HoganFirst Appearance"Nevermore" Warren Beringis the father of Warehouse AgentMyka Bering. He is part owner of the Colorado Springs, CO bookstore "Bering and Sons" and first appeared in the episodeNevermore. He was mentioned in an earlier episode, whenPeteand Myka discussed their backgrounds one evening atLeena's Bed and Breakfast. Mr. Bering became a rather unwilling stand-in for Edgar Allen Poe's pen, when the disorientated companion notebook to which the pen was normally attached became attracted to him. At first he appeared to show no ill effect and the notebook released him shortly after he touched it, but when he touched it and read some of it the second time, the notebook sense his love for the written word. Desperately wanting to be reunited with its missing pen, the notebook poured its words into Mr. Bering, literally, thus using him has a form of substitute for its partner until they could be reunited. The two separated artifacts then attempted to communicate with each other through Warren using a series of visions so they could help each other find each other.

characters.name: Davis Braid
characters.description: Father Davis BraidPriestGenderMaleLocationUnionville, COAffiliationCatholic ChurchStatusAlivePlayed byPhillip CraigFirst Appearance"Magnetism"FatherDavis Braidis a Catholic Priest at a Church in Unionville, Colorado. He is a direct descendant of James Braid. Father Braid is first seen at his Church when talking to agent Myka Bering. His congregation included all of those affected byJames Braid's Chair(Meg Riley,Grace Ellen, Sheriff "Mac" Mackenna,Tommy Wilson,Ross, etc.) and many more. When Mac went crazy due to the chair, he was evacuated, and his chair along with the part of the church was destroyed.

characters.name: Brenda
characters.description: Brenda"Hit the bricks, slick."ManagerGenderFemaleRaceBurmese[1]LocationUniville, South DakotaAffiliationUnited States Postal ServiceStatusAlivePlayed byKyra HarperFirst Appearance"Mild Mannered"Last Appearance"Beyond Our Control" Brendais the manager of the Univille Post Office. Brenda first appears in "Mild Mannered", being the employee whom Myka and Pete meet at the post office, when they go to pick up their belongings from Washington D.C. However only Myka's things have been shipped and Pete is left without anything.

characters.name: Walter Burleigh
characters.description: Walter BurleighGenderMaleStatusDeceasedFamilyUnnamed wifeLacell(nephew)First AppearanceElements Walter Burleighwas creator of the four artifacts/pieces on the Rock, Wind, Fire, and Water.

characters.name: Bobby Buseki
characters.description: Bobby BusekiBobby BusekiGenderMaleRaceCaucasianLocationPortland, ORAffiliationKingford AcademyStatusAlivePlayed byAlec MedlockFirst Appearance"Nevermore" Bobby Busekiwas a normal guy in high school. As he passes Tamara in the schoolyard, he smiles. When he gets into his school, he passesGregin the hallway. Greg takes his stuff and finds a picture of Tamara; he then rips up the photo. This makes Bobby mad. Later in his classroom he hears whispering. Before Bobby leaves, his teacher gives him a book of poems. He fixes his photo of Tamara. But he continues to hears whispers, leading him to steal Edgar Allen Poe's pen, which is in a display case. He burns Greg with fire, using to power of the pen to bring written words into reality. When confronted by his teacher about the theft of the pen, he puts the teacher into the wall; he later clames Tamara as his. Warehouse agentsPete LattimerandClaudia Donovanshow up at the school and begin asking questions. Bobby then traps the two of them by tying them up and placing them under a swinging axe blade. Eventually, Edgar Allen Poe's pen is snagged and bagged by Claudia, releasing Bobby from its effects.

characters.name: Jeff Canning
characters.description: Jeff Canning preparing to talk about Eric Played by Elias Zarou inResonance. He's the owner of Windy Lake Records as a company, and the current (reluctant) custodian ofEric Marsden's musical legacy, which he will happily offload in its entirety for any reasonable offer.

characters.name: Jeff Canning
characters.description: Jeff Canning preparing to talk about Eric Played by Elias Zarou inResonance. He's the owner of Windy Lake Records as a company, and the current (reluctant) custodian ofEric Marsden's musical legacy, which he will happily offload in its entirety for any reasonable offer.

characters.name: Caturanga
characters.description: CaturangaAgent-in-Charge ofWarehouse 12GenderMaleRaceIndianLocationLondon, EnglandAffiliationWarehouse 12StatusDeceasedPlayed byErick AvariFirst AppearanceEmily Lake(Mentioned)"Stand"Last Appearance"Endless" Caturangawas the Agent-in-Charge ofWarehouse 12in London, and was, among other things, responsible for the training ofHelena G. Wellsas an apprentice and agent of the Warehouse. No records describe of Caturanga's life before the Warehouse, but only that circumstances led him to being appointed a supervisory position at Warehouse 12, very much in the same capacity asArtieatWarehouse 13. As well, he was part of the Warehouse personnel who armed theRegent's Sanctumin Hong Kong, creating a deadly lock based on his favorite game, chess.[1] Caturanga was an eccentric and friendly man with a seemingly deep connection to Warehouse 12.[1]He and H.G. seemed to get along very well, and for all intents and purposes worked superbly as partners.[2] His favorite game was chess, which he played every day. H.G. often challenged him to a match, but never won a single game against him.[1]

H.G. often challenged him to a match, but never won a single game against him.[1]

characters.name: Abigail Chow
characters.description: Abigail ChowOwner of Bed and BreakfastKeeperGenderFemaleRaceChinese/Native HawaiianAliases or NicknamesAbiDr. ChowLocationUniville, South Dakota (last known; presumably current)AffiliationWarehouse 13,Leena's Bed and BreakfastStatusAliveQuirksPsychotherapyWarehouse KeeperPlayed byKelly HuFirst Appearance"The Sky's the Limit"Last Appearance"Endless Terror"  Abigail Chow[1]is the new owner and operator of theBed and Breakfastto replaceLeena. She was a psychotherapist, then photographer, before she was brought to the B&B. She was asked by theRegentsto help Artie cope with his grief, and in general to aid in the psychological well-being of Warehouse agents. She is also theKeeperof the history of the Warehouse for her generation.[2] Abigail appears to be very determined when work has to be done, and quick to learn and find solutions to complicated problems. She's someone who doesn't get frighten easily as well (as mentioned in "The Sky's the Limit" where she introduced herself to Artie). However, she also seems to be haunted by the memory of the patient she couldn't help, rattling her self-confidence and making her doubt her ability to assist anyone.

characters.name: City engineer
characters.description: Played by Roop Gill inBurnout.

characters.name: Claire
characters.description: Claire (left) as Lee sees her. Played by Lara Amersey inRegrets. She was apparently killed in a drink-driving incident byLee Donaldson, who was in turn hounded to his death by the vision of her he saw in theRiverton Maximum Security Prison.

characters.name: Claire
characters.description: Claire (left) as Lee sees her. Played by Lara Amersey inRegrets. She was apparently killed in a drink-driving incident byLee Donaldson, who was in turn hounded to his death by the vision of her he saw in theRiverton Maximum Security Prison.

characters.name: Clark
characters.description: Sergeant ClarkPolice OfficerGenderMaleRaceCaucasianLocationSt. Louis, MissouriAffiliationSt. Louis Police Department Gang UnitStatusDeceasedHealthKilled through use of theSpine of the SaracenFirst AppearanceBurnoutLast AppearanceBurnout SergeantClarkwas the head of the St. Louis' precinct's gang unit where several gang members had been electrocuted. However, when Myka and Pete began their investigation, he was away on leave. He turned out to be the one who had found theSpine of the Saracen, whichJack Secordhad hoped to entomb with himself. The rage it provoked was, in Clark's case, directed against gangs, but as with all users except Pete, he was eventually 'consumed' by the Spine itself, and died.

characters.name: Agt. Cooper
characters.description: Agent Cooper is one of the Homeland Security Agents assigned to 'escort' Myka and Pete out of D.C. after they get caught up in Artie's attempts to retrieve theHonjo Masamuneand its tsuba. Played by British actorArnold PinnockinImplosion.
